Kaine, Sen. Duckworth quoted); War Powers Resolution May 1 60-day statutory deadline T-1; H.Con.Res.38 (Massie/Khanna) at 94 cosponsors per congress.gov, latest Senate vote Apr 15 failed 52-47 (4th failure); Hegseth Apr 29 framing of congressional critics as 'biggest adversary' + Cooper strike-options briefing indicate WH positioning to argue post-May 1 kinetic action under Article II not WPR-derived authority; Iran reactivates 30-yr-old VLCC for floating storage (Kpler 12-22 days capacity left); Iran gov Apr 29 instructs offices to cut electricity 70% after 1 p.m. (Oil Min Paknejad: conservation 'religious duty'); active escalation risk indicator: ELEVATED

Susan Collins (R-ME) joins Democrats ('is not a suggestion; it is a requirement'); Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) threatens AUMF when Senate returns from recess week of May 11; Sen. Mike Rounds (R-SD) expects Trump to seek 30-day extension; Congress enters week-long recess; no Democratic lawsuit filed as of publication; Iran transmits updated peace proposal via Pakistani mediators (IRNA); Brent settles −5% at $108.10 (vs Apr 30 close $114.22) and WTI −4% at $101.05 paring week's geopolitical premium; Trump reaffirms blockade continues 'until [Tehran] cries uncle'; S&P 500 trades to fresh intraday/closing record ~7,210 (+0.5%) on first day of May, capping strongest monthly rally since 2020 (April best month since 2020); Nasdaq ~25,000 (+1.0%) record; Apple +~4% on iPhone 17 demand and Q2 earnings beat; Exxon/Chevron beat EPS but report -45%/-36% net income on oil-shock cycle; Spirit Aviation Holdings preparing to cease operations after government bailout talks failed (WSJ); AAA national gas $4.392/gal (state-averages page; +47% vs $2.99 baseline; AAA Mid-Atlantic spokesperson conflictingly says weekly average 'went down'); Adm. Cooper (CENTCOM) briefed Trump Apr 30 on next-phase options including 'limited ground interventions, targeted strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure' (SOF News expansion of Axios 'short and intense wave' framing); CENTCOM Apr 29 Dark Eagle hypersonic-weapon AOR request remains pending; ~150 Soldiers HHC 192nd MP Battalion (Connecticut Army National Guard) short-notice ME deployment Apr 28 (DVIDS); State Department 'Maritime Freedom Construct' cable Apr 28 to U.S. embassies; USS Gerald R. label: MAY 3, 2026 — DAY 65
headline: Trump reviews Iran 14-pt counter-proposal delivered via Pakistan May 2 (Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirms; 30-day deadline; end of war 'on all fronts including Lebanon'; troop withdrawal; blockade lift; sanctions relief; 'new mechanism for the Strait of Hormuz') — Trump in Florida pre-AF1: 'I cannot imagine that it would be acceptable'; Iran 'has not paid a big enough price for what they have done over the last 47 years'; left open resumption: 'If they do something bad, there is a possibility it could happen.' IRGC May 3 (Al Jazeera): 'room for U.S. decision-making has narrowed'; Trump 'must choose between an impossible military operation or a bad deal.' State Dept fast-tracks $8.6B arms package to Israel/Qatar/Kuwait/UAE under Rubio emergency waiver bypassing congressional review (Qatar Patriot $4.01B + APKWS $992.4M; Kuwait IBCS $2.5B; Israel APKWS $992.4M; UAE APKWS $147.6M). CENTCOM Cdr Adm. Brad Cooper visits USS Tripoli (LHA-7) in Arabian Sea May 2 (@CENTCOM X) — strategic-communication signal timed to counter-proposal delivery. CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirects 48 vessels (CBS via CENTCOM May 2; +3 in past 20 hours). Iran condemns capture of tankers Majestic X and Tifani as 'armed robbery on the high seas' (FM spokesperson Baqaei: Trump 'pirates' remark a 'damning admission'). Iran parliament National Security Committee finalizes 12-article 'Sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz' law for chamber vote on Majlis return — permanent ban on Israeli vessels; rial-payment requirement; 20% cargo confiscation penalty. Lebanon front structurally widens: IDF May 3 issues evacuation orders for towns NORTH of Litani — first major geographic expansion since Apr 17 ceasefire took effect; IDF kills 2 Hezbollah fighters posing 'imminent threat'; Hezbollah rocket/drone fire (no Israeli injuries). Saturday IDF strike package: ~70 military structures + 50 Hezbollah sites dismantled per IDF; Lebanese MoPH cumulative 2,659 killed / 8,183 wounded (+41 / +89 vs Day 63); deaths in Shoukine, Kfar Dajjal, Lwaizeh, Habboush, Nabatieh, Tyre district. LAF Cdr Haykal meets U.S. Gen. Joseph Clearfield (head of 5-member ceasefire monitoring committee) May 2/3. AAA gas $4.446 (+5.4¢ d/d, +48.7% vs $2.99 baseline; ~30¢/wk; CA $6.10; HI $5.634); markets closed Sunday — Brent $108.17 / WTI $101.94 (Friday carryforward); S&P 500 7,230.12 (Fri close, record); Nasdaq 25,114.44; Dow 49,499.27. Per manifest 4a / discrepancy #1, USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) location updated to Eastern Mediterranean per USNI Apr 27 + Apr 20 (dashboard 'Red Sea' label was stale); Lincoln Arabian Sea blockade ops; Bush off coast of Africa heading to 5th Fleet — 3-CSG-plus-LHA Tripoli concentration first since OIF 2003. Trump May 3 signals German troop drawdown will go 'a lot further' than 5,000 announced May 1; House and Senate Armed Services GOP chairs 'very concerned'; planned long-range fires battalion deployment cancelled. Iran ceasefire holds Day 25 (Day 12 of indefinite extension; zero kinetic strikes since 8 PM ET Apr 7; Bushehr NPP ~29 consecutive days no incident). U.S. KIA 15 (all-cause; 13 combat-only Pentagon official); WIA 415 (carry-forward). Iran cumulative casualty data STALE across 4 channels: Foundation of Martyrs 3,468 (Apr 19, 14 days); Iran MoH 3,375 (mid-April); HRANA 3,636 (Apr 7); Iran forensics chief ~3,400 (NBC late-April). Cumulative U.S. cost midpoint $54.345B; Pentagon Comptroller Hurst official $25B (Apr 29 HASC); range to ~$1T (CSIS upper bound incl. economic spillover). Sunday-data caveat: ~60% of Day 65 quantitative dashboard updates are carry-forward (markets closed; USNI Fleet Tracker last refreshed Apr 27); next refresh Mon May 4. WPR endgame T-8 days to Senate return week of May 11 — Sen. Murkowski (R-AK) AUMF remains sole live legislative vehicle; Sen. Susan Collins (R-ME) crossed over Apr 30 (50-47, 6th failure). Active escalation risk: ELEVATED — Lebanon expansion + IRGC 'narrowed window' + Murkowski AUMF threat.

label: MAY 4, 2026 — DAY 66
headline: Project Freedom kicks off; Iran retaliates with missile/drone barrage on UAE; Brent +5.8% to $114.44, oil's biggest single-day jump since early April
